title: Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation)
version: Official Journal text; current consolidated status must be checked on EUR-Lex
summary: EU-wide rules governing the processing of personal data and the protection of natural persons.
statusNote: In force and generally applicable since 25 May 2018.

source.label: EUR-Lex

textAvailability.note: Complete Article records are generated separately from EUR-Lex in gdpr-articles.json.

title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence
version: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 Official Journal text; Digital Omnibus on AI finally adopted in June 2026 with final text PE 30 2026 REV 1, pending an Official Journal legal-act record in this snapshot
summary: Risk-tiered EU rules for placing AI systems on the market, putting them into service, deploying them, and governing general-purpose AI models.
statusNote: In force with phased application under the 2024 text. Chapters I and II applied from 2 February 2025 and further listed provisions from 2 August 2025. The Digital Omnibus on AI received final Council approval on 29 June 2026 and its final legislative text is dated 8 July 2026; this snapshot does not apply its revised dates until publication and entry into force are confirmed in the Official Journal.

source.label: EUR-Lex

textAvailability.note: Complete Article records are generated separately from EUR-Lex in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

amendmentSource.label: EUR-Lex — Digital Omnibus on AI final legislative text, PE 30 2026 REV 1

title: Executive Order 14110 — Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence
version: Historical Federal Register text
summary: A former whole-of-government AI order that directed federal agencies on safety testing, reporting, standards, civil rights, procurement, and sectoral uses.
statusNote: Revoked on 20 January 2025 by Executive Order 14148. It is retained as a historical policy node, not presented as current law.

source.label: Federal Register

textAvailability.note: The complete Order is stored from the GovInfo official electronic Federal Register edition. Every node is labelled revoked historical text, not a current duty.

amendmentSource.label: Executive Order 14148 revoking Executive Order 14110 — Federal Register

title: Executive Order 14179 — Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence
version: Federal Register text
summary: Sets the executive branch policy of sustaining and enhancing US AI leadership and directed preparation of an AI Action Plan and review of actions taken under revoked EO 14110.
statusNote: EO 14179 remains in force within its executive-branch scope, but it is not the whole current federal AI-policy picture and is not a private-sector AI statute. Later executive orders, national-security directives, and unenacted legislative recommendations must be modeled as separate instruments rather than silently incorporated here.

source.label: Federal Register

textAvailability.note: The complete current Order is stored from the GovInfo official electronic Federal Register edition.

title: SB 1047 (2023–2024) — Safe and Secure Innovation for Frontier Artificial Intelligence Models Act
version: Enrolled bill version published 3 September 2024; vetoed 29 September 2024 and never enacted
summary: A California frontier-model safety bill that reached the Governor but did not become law.
statusNote: Vetoed by the Governor on 29 September 2024. Proposed duties must never be displayed as current California law.

source.label: California Legislative Information

supportingSource.label: California Legislative Information — enrolled SB 1047 text

textAvailability.note: The complete enrolled bill body is stored in 18 navigable nodes. Every node is labelled vetoed, never enacted, never commenced, and historical only.

title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
issuingBodies: National Institute of Standards and Technology
summary: A voluntary, rights-preserving, non-sector-specific framework organized around GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, and MANAGE functions.
statusNote: AI RMF 1.0 remains NIST's current published voluntary core framework while a revision is in progress. Profiles and the Playbook are separate resources and do not silently replace the core or establish legal compliance.

source.label: NIST

textAvailability.note: The complete NIST AI 100-1 publication is stored in 135 hierarchical text nodes. Identified ISO/OECD material remains excluded from the project license and any blanket public-domain claim.

amendmentSource.label: NIST AI Risk Management Framework hub — current framework and revision status

supportingSource.label: NIST AI 100-1 — controlling complete official edition

summary: China's comprehensive national law governing the processing and protection of personal information.

summary: China's foundational national cybersecurity law, including network-operation security, information protection, monitoring, and enforcement provisions.

title: United Kingdom General Data Protection Regulation
issuingBodies: European Parliament and Council of the European Union (original regulation)
Parliament of the United Kingdom (assimilation and later amendment)
version: UK GDPR as amended, including most Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 Part 5 changes effective 5 February 2026 and section 103 effective 19 June 2026
summary: The UK data-protection regulation derived from the EU GDPR and operating with the Data Protection Act 2018, as amended.
statusNote: The UK GDPR remains a distinct UK instrument. Most DUAA 2025 Part 5 privacy changes took effect on 5 February 2026 and section 103 on controller complaint procedures on 19 June 2026; use the legislation.gov.uk timeline for point-in-time research and do not assume identity with EU GDPR.

source.label: UK Legislation

textAvailability.note: Use the revised-text and timeline controls on legislation.gov.uk for version-sensitive research.

amendmentSource.label: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 6) Regulations 2026

referenceTranslationSource.label: Japanese Law Translation — government reference translation

title: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

title: Directive on Automated Decision-Making
version: Current page modified 2025-06-24; transition to updated requirements ended 2026-06-24
summary: Mandatory federal-government policy requirements for automated systems used to make or support administrative decisions within its institutional and operational scope.
statusNote: Mandatory only within its federal-policy scope, not an Act of Parliament or a general private-sector AI law. The transition to the requirements reflected on the page modified 24 June 2025 ended on 24 June 2026.

title: Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
version: Act No. 22 of 2023 with phased commencement notification G.S.R. 843(E)
summary: India's national statute for processing digital personal data, with institutional provisions and substantive obligations commencing on different dates.
statusNote: As of 20 July 2026, specified institutional and rulemaking provisions are in force. Most core processing duties and rights are scheduled for 14 May 2027; one intermediate group is scheduled for 14 November 2026. Every local unit carries its own phase status.

source.label: Gazette of India — Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023

textAvailability.note: All 44 Sections and the complete Schedule are stored from the official Gazette enactment. Each unit is layered with the official three-phase commencement notification rather than presented as uniformly operative.

title: Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025

summary: Implementation rules under the DPDP Act covering notices, consent managers, safeguards, breach notices, rights, children, significant data fiduciaries, and Board procedure through phased commencement.
statusNote: Rules 1, 2, and 17–21 took effect on publication; Rule 4 is scheduled one year later; Rules 3, 5–16, 22, and 23 are scheduled eighteen months after publication. The local corpus applies the official 16 December 2025 corrigendum.

source.label: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology / Gazette of India

summary: G7 international guiding principles and a code of conduct for advanced AI systems, developed through the Hiroshima AI Process.

title: Governing AI for Humanity — Final Report of the UN Secretary-General's High-level Advisory Body on AI
issuingBodies: United Nations Secretary-General's High-level Advisory Body on Artificial Intelligence
version: Final report, September 2024
summary: An expert advisory report proposing seven institutional recommendations for inclusive global AI governance.
statusNote: Advisory report rather than a UN treaty, resolution, or binding legal instrument. The UN supplies Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian and Spanish editions; later UN governance initiatives are separate instruments, not amendments to this report.

source.label: United Nations Digital Library — Governing AI for Humanity

textAvailability.note: The UN record links Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian and Spanish publication editions. The local index and editorial summaries use English; that local choice is not a claim that English controls the other editions.

title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system
version: Edition 1, 2023
summary: An international requirements standard for establishing, operating, maintaining, and continually improving an organization-wide AI management system that addresses AI risks and opportunities.
statusNote: Published international standard. Its requirements are not automatically law; obligations can arise through adoption, certification, contracts, procurement, or another applicable legal regime.

source.label: ISO

textAvailability.note: ISO controls access to the copyrighted standard. This dataset stores only original editorial metadata and links the official access and preview page.

title: Ethically Aligned Design: A Vision for Prioritizing Human Well-being with Autonomous and Intelligent Systems
version: First Edition, 2019
summary: A human-centred design framework for surfacing ethical values, rights, well-being, accountability, and societal impacts when autonomous and intelligent systems are designed and governed.
statusNote: Published IEEE white paper and voluntary ethical-design framework, not a binding law or an IEEE technical standard. Later IEEE standards and programs are separate instruments.

source.label: IEEE Standards Association

textAvailability.note: The copyrighted publication is not reproduced. This dataset provides original high-level summaries and links the official IEEE access page.

title: Recommendation of the Council on Artificial Intelligence (OECD/LEGAL/0449)
version: OECD/LEGAL/0449, amended 3 May 2024
summary: An intergovernmental recommendation combining five values-based principles for trustworthy AI with five policy recommendations for governments, updated for recent technological and policy developments.
statusNote: Active OECD recommendation, amended on 3 May 2024. It is an influential non-binding legal instrument and does not itself create directly enforceable domestic duties.

source.label: OECD Legal Instruments

textAvailability.note: The current recommendation is linked from the official OECD Legal Instruments service.

title: The Bletchley Declaration by Countries Attending the AI Safety Summit, 1–2 November 2023
issuingBodies: Countries and organizations attending the AI Safety Summit 2023
version: Declaration issued 1 November 2023; official participant list updated 13 February 2025 to record New Zealand's 23 October 2024 accession
summary: A multilateral political declaration framing frontier-AI risks as a shared international concern and calling for scientific understanding, risk-based policy, safety evaluation, transparency, and sustained cooperation.
statusNote: Non-binding political declaration rather than a treaty or domestic law. The official page was later updated to record an additional signatory without changing its legal force.

source.label: UK Government

textAvailability.note: The official declaration is linked from GOV.UK; this seed stores an editorial summary rather than a duplicate of the text.

title: Personal Data Protection Act 2012 (Act 26 of 2012)
version: 2020 Revised Edition, legal version effective 5 December 2025; SSO currency verified 20 July 2026
summary: Singapore's generally applicable private-sector personal-data statute, organized around consent, purpose, access and correction, protection, retention, transfer, accountability, and breach-notification duties.
statusNote: The Act commenced in phases and remains in force as amended. The linked Singapore Statutes Online version and its provision-level timeline control version-sensitive research.

source.label: Singapore Statutes Online, Attorney-General's Chambers

textAvailability.note: All 95 current section slots and 11 Schedules are stored from the Singapore Statutes Online consolidation as at 20 July 2026. SSO states that its consolidated reproduction is unofficial and not authoritative. Reproduction attribution and latest-version checking conditions are preserved in the corpus metadata.

title: Model Artificial Intelligence Governance Framework, Second Edition
version: Second Edition (21 January 2020)
summary: A sector- and technology-agnostic implementation framework for internal governance, appropriate human involvement, operations management, and stakeholder communication in organizational AI deployment.
statusNote: This is voluntary guidance, not legislation or certification. Specialized later Singapore frameworks for generative and agentic AI supplement rather than silently amend this edition.

source.label: Personal Data Protection Commission Singapore

textAvailability.note: The official landing page and complete Second Edition PDF are linked; only structured editorial framework nodes are stored locally.

title: AI Verify
version: International MVP launched 25 May 2022; open-source foundation launched 7 June 2023; status verified 19 July 2026
summary: An open-source AI governance testing framework and toolkit combining technical tests and process checks across internationally recognized responsible-AI principles.
statusNote: AI Verify supports evidence generation and assurance workflows but neither sets binding ethical standards nor guarantees that a tested system is safe, unbiased, or legally compliant.

source.label: Infocomm Media Development Authority

amendmentSource.label: IMDA — launch of the AI Verify Foundation and open-source phase

textAvailability.note: Official framework information and the open-source toolkit are linked; this graph stores an editorial capability map rather than software or test specifications.

version: Version 1.0, September 2023
summary: SDAIA's national responsible-AI principles and associated self-assessment pathway for organizations developing or deploying AI in Saudi Arabia.
statusNote: Version 1.0 uses both mandatory and recommendatory language and assigns SDAIA compliance-monitoring roles, while registration is described as optional. Applicability and binding effect must be checked by actor and risk tier.

source.label: Saudi Data and Artificial Intelligence Authority — verified English framework

textAvailability.note: The verified official English framework and SDAIA assessment service are linked. The Arabic title is retained, but a matching complete Arabic PDF is not claimed until separately verified.

title: Personal Data Protection Act
originalTitle: 個人資料保護法
version: Current Ministry of Justice consolidation; specified 2023 and 11 November 2025 amendments remain uncommenced
summary: Taiwan's generally applicable personal-data statute for public and non-public agencies, covering collection, processing, use, rights, security, supervision, and liability.
statusNote: The statute descends from the 1995 Computer-Processed Personal Data Protection Act; its renamed current-form framework generally took effect on 1 October 2012. Promulgation is not commencement: Article 1-1 and specified 2025 amendments await dates set by the Executive Yuan, and the Personal Data Protection Commission remains in preparatory status.

source.label: Ministry of Justice Laws and Regulations Database — Chinese

referenceTranslationSource.label: Ministry of Justice Laws and Regulations Database — English

textAvailability.note: All 66 Article nodes from the official latest Traditional-Chinese consolidation and government English reference translation are stored. Each node distinguishes in-force text from the uncommenced 2025 package; affected Articles retain the current-effective prior Chinese text.

title: Artificial Intelligence Basic Act
originalTitle: 人工智慧基本法
version: Promulgated 14 January 2026, 20 Articles
summary: Taiwan's enacted AI framework law, establishing policy principles and government responsibilities for human-centric, risk-aware, secure, transparent, fair, and accountable AI development and use.
statusNote: This is a promulgated and effective 20-Article law, not a draft. Many operational requirements depend on competent-authority measures and other applicable laws.

source.label: National Science and Technology Council — official Chinese text

referenceTranslationSource.label: National Science and Technology Council — official English text

textAvailability.note: All 20 Articles are stored from the official Traditional-Chinese publication together with the National Science and Technology Council's English reference translation.

title: Reference Guidelines for the Use of Generative AI by the Executive Yuan and Its Subordinate Agencies (Institutions)
originalTitle: 行政院及所屬機關（構）使用生成式AI參考指引
version: Official Traditional-Chinese guidance dispatched 3 October 2023; no official revision located through 20 July 2026
summary: Practical public-sector guidance on generative-AI use, including protection of confidential and personal information, human verification, attribution, accountability, and risk-sensitive restrictions.
statusNote: The guidelines are directed to the Executive Yuan and subordinate bodies and are not a generally applicable private-sector statute. No complete official English version was located.

source.label: National Science and Technology Council — current official guidance record

supportingSource.label: Official issuance notice recording the 3 October 2023 dispatch

textAvailability.note: The complete four-paragraph general explanation and all ten numbered points are stored from the official Traditional-Chinese ODT. No complete official English translation was located; English reader orientation is editorial only.

title: Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (Cap. 486)
originalTitle: 《個人資料（私隱）條例》（第486章）
version: Hong Kong e-Legislation current-version dataset, whole-chapter version date 1 October 2022, retrieved 20 July 2026
summary: Hong Kong's personal-data privacy ordinance, structured around six Data Protection Principles, data-subject rights, enforcement, exemptions, and later anti-doxxing provisions.
statusNote: The English and Chinese texts are co-authentic and have equal status. Section 33's restriction on transfers outside Hong Kong is enacted but not in operation and is not presented as a current duty; sections 34, 72 and 73 remain explicit repeal or spent placeholders.

source.label: Hong Kong e-Legislation — bilingual official text

textAvailability.note: All 124 Sections and six Schedules are stored in complete co-authentic English and Traditional Chinese. The interface defaults to English and offers the equal-status Chinese text on demand; source-version dates are not substituted for commencement dates.

title: Artificial Intelligence: Model Personal Data Protection Framework
originalTitle: 《人工智能（AI）：個人資料保障模範框架》
issuingBodies: Office of the Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data, Hong Kong
version: Issued 11 June 2024
summary: The Hong Kong privacy regulator's model framework for procuring, implementing, and operating AI while protecting personal data through governance, risk assessment, customization, management, and communication measures.
statusNote: This is regulator-issued voluntary guidance that helps operationalize PDPO responsibilities; it is not itself an ordinance or a certification of compliance.

source.label: Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data, Hong Kong

textAvailability.note: The verified official English framework PDF and release are linked. A Chinese edition is not claimed without a separately evidenced official publication; the graph stores editorial navigation nodes rather than reproducing the document.

title: Guidance on the Ethical Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence
originalTitle: 《開發及使用人工智能道德標準指引》
issuingBodies: Office of the Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data, Hong Kong
version: August 2021
summary: Hong Kong privacy-regulator guidance translating ethical AI values into governance and lifecycle practices for organizations that develop or use AI involving personal data.
statusNote: This is non-binding regulatory guidance and predates the more operational 2024 Model Personal Data Protection Framework; both remain distinct research nodes.

source.label: Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data, Hong Kong

textAvailability.note: The official English guidance PDF is linked; the local record contains original summaries rather than copied full publication text.

title: Checklist on Guidelines for the Use of Generative AI by Employees
originalTitle: 《僱員使用生成式AI的指引清單》
issuingBodies: Office of the Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data, Hong Kong
version: Issued 31 March 2025
summary: A practical Hong Kong workplace checklist for organizational generative-AI policies, including permitted tools and uses, input restrictions, output verification, security, incident reporting, and employee training.
statusNote: The checklist is practical voluntary guidance for employers and employees; it does not create a new statutory cause of action or replace the PDPO.

source.label: Privacy Commissioner for Personal Data, Hong Kong

textAvailability.note: The official release and checklist are linked; the repository stores a structured summary of policy elements rather than the full checklist.

title: Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection
originalTitle: Undang-Undang Nomor 27 Tahun 2022 tentang Pelindungan Data Pribadi
issuingBodies: President of the Republic of Indonesia
version: Official Indonesian text; Article 53 affected by Constitutional Court Decision 151/PUU-XXII/2024 on 30 July 2025
summary: Indonesia's comprehensive personal-data protection statute, covering processing bases, controller and processor duties, rights, impact assessment, breach notification, data-protection officers, transfers, and sanctions.
statusNote: The Law took effect on enactment and its two-year adjustment period ended in October 2024. Article 53 must be read with the 2025 Constitutional Court decision; no complete official English text was located.

source.label: Government of Indonesia legislation portal

supportingSource.label: Ministry JDIH — official Indonesian HTML text

textAvailability.note: All 76 Articles are stored in Indonesian from the controlling official Gazette and official JDIH text, with disclosed OCR/list-marker verification. Article 53 preserves the promulgated wording and separately stores the Constitutional Court's binding current interpretation. No complete official English translation is claimed.

title: Law on Personal Data Protection (Law No. 91/2025/QH15)
originalTitle: Luật Bảo vệ dữ liệu cá nhân (Luật số 91/2025/QH15)
version: Law No. 91/2025/QH15, implemented with Decree No. 356/2025/ND-CP
summary: Viet Nam's current personal-data protection statute, replacing a decree-centered framework with national legislation and detailed implementing rules from 1 January 2026.
statusNote: The 2025 Law and Decree 356 form the current regime. It is inaccurate to present repealed Decree 13 as current or to reduce the regime to a blanket data-localization rule.

source.label: Government of Viet Nam — official law metadata and signed text

textAvailability.note: All 39 Articles are stored in official Vietnamese verified against the signed National Assembly text and searchable Official Gazette. No complete official English version was verified or generated.

title: Decree No. 356/2025/ND-CP Detailing Articles of and Measures for Implementing the Law on Personal Data Protection
originalTitle: Nghị định số 356/2025/NĐ-CP quy định chi tiết một số điều và biện pháp thi hành Luật Bảo vệ dữ liệu cá nhân
version: Decree No. 356/2025/ND-CP
summary: The current implementing decree for Viet Nam's 2025 Personal Data Protection Law, supplying detailed procedures, assessment documentation, safeguards, and transition rules.
statusNote: Decree 356 took effect with the Law on 1 January 2026 and expressly replaced Decree 13/2023; the parent statute and implementing decree must be read together.

source.label: Government of Viet Nam — official decree text

textAvailability.note: All 42 Articles and 13 Appendix forms are stored in official Vietnamese verified against the signed Government text and searchable Official Gazette. No complete official English version was verified or generated.

title: Decree No. 13/2023/ND-CP on Personal Data Protection
originalTitle: Nghị định số 13/2023/NĐ-CP về bảo vệ dữ liệu cá nhân
version: Historical Decree No. 13/2023/ND-CP, repealed by Article 42(2) of Decree No. 356/2025/ND-CP
summary: Viet Nam's former decree-centered personal-data regime, retained to explain the transition to the 2025 Law and Decree 356 rather than presented as current law.
statusNote: This Decree was repealed from 1 January 2026. Any current compliance description that cites it without the replacement Law and Decree 356 is stale.

source.label: Government of Viet Nam — historical official decree text

textAvailability.note: All 44 Articles and six Appendix forms are stored in historical official Vietnamese. The Decree is labelled repealed from 1 January 2026 throughout; no complete official English text is claimed.

title: Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013
version: Current Department of Justice consolidation of Act 4 of 2013; phased commencement and section 58(2) application reviewed through 20 July 2026
summary: South Africa's comprehensive personal-information statute, establishing eight processing conditions, data-subject rights, regulator powers, security and breach duties, prior authorization, and transfer rules.
statusNote: POPIA commenced in tranches; the general compliance grace period ended on 30 June 2021. Section 58(2) commenced on 1 July 2021, but its application to processing under section 57 was postponed until 1 February 2022.

source.label: South African Department of Justice — current consolidation

textAvailability.note: All 115 Sections and the complete Schedule are stored from the Department of Justice current consolidation, excluding publisher page furniture and editorial annotations. Provision-level commencement metadata preserves the phased implementation record.

title: Nigeria Data Protection Act, 2023 (Act No. 37 of 2023)
issuingBodies: President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria
version: Act No. 37 of 2023; official Gazette text reviewed as in force and unamended through 20 July 2026
summary: Nigeria's federal personal-data statute, establishing the Nigeria Data Protection Commission and rules for lawful processing, rights, governance, impact assessment, security, breaches, and cross-border transfers.
statusNote: The Act entered into force on presidential assent. Amendment bills identified in National Assembly records had not become amending Acts as of 20 July 2026; subsidiary instruments remain separate and should be checked for implementation details.

source.label: Nigeria Data Protection Commission

supportingSource.label: Nigeria Data Protection Commission — official Gazette copy

textAvailability.note: All 66 Sections and the complete Schedule (paragraphs 1–18) are stored from the official Gazette copy hosted by the Nigeria Data Protection Commission.

title: Bill C-27 — Digital Charter Implementation Act, 2022, Part 3: Artificial Intelligence and Data Act
version: 44th Parliament, 1st Session bill; lapsed on prorogation and never received Royal Assent
summary: The lapsed Canadian Bill C-27 included the proposed Artificial Intelligence and Data Act for commercial high-impact AI, risk mitigation, records, transparency, audits, and enforcement.
statusNote: AIDA never became law and lapsed when the parliamentary session ended in January 2025. A later Bill C-27 in another Parliament is unrelated and must not be confused with it.

source.label: Parliament of Canada — LEGISinfo

textAvailability.note: The official bilingual bill dossier and text are linked. Proposed AIDA provisions are indexed solely as historical proposal nodes.

title: SB 24-205 — Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence
version: 2024 enactment; delayed by SB 25B-004 and repealed/re-enacted through SB 26-189 before principal duties operated
summary: Colorado's 2024 high-risk AI enactment targeting algorithmic discrimination, preserved as the historical source of a regulatory lineage later delayed and re-enacted in materially revised form.
statusNote: It is inaccurate to describe SB 24-205 as simply becoming operative in 2026. The current forward-looking node is SB 26-189, whose principal duties begin in 2027.

source.label: Colorado General Assembly

textAvailability.note: Official enrolled text, history, and fiscal materials are linked. This node is historical and not the current operative compliance text.

title: SB 26-189 — Automated Decision-Making Technology
version: Colorado Session Laws 2026, chapter 131; principal obligations effective 1 January 2027
summary: Colorado's current enacted framework for automated decision-making technology used materially in consequential decisions, with developer documentation, deployer notices, consumer rights, records, and Attorney General enforcement.
statusNote: Specified provisions took effect on signature on 14 May 2026, while the principal regulated-actor duties apply to consequential decisions made on or after 1 January 2027. The session law repeals and re-enacts the earlier SB 24-205 pathway.

source.label: Colorado General Assembly

textAvailability.note: All 18 navigable units of Session Laws 2026, chapter 131 are stored. Unit-level records distinguish provisions effective on passage from principal duties and consequential-decision applicability beginning 1 January 2027.

title: Federal Act of 25 September 2020 on Data Protection (Data Protection Act, FADP)
originalTitle: Bundesgesetz über den Datenschutz (Datenschutzgesetz, DSG)
version: Fedlex SR 235.1 consolidation applicable from 7 July 2025; general commencement remains 1 September 2023
summary: Switzerland's revised federal data-protection statute, providing technology-neutral processing principles, privacy by design and default, security, impact assessment, breach notification, rights, and international-transfer controls.
statusNote: The revised FADP has applied since 1 September 2023. It is a technology-neutral privacy statute, not a crypto, Web3, or AI-specific regulatory code.

source.label: Fedlex — official non-authoritative English translation

originalLanguageSource.label: Fedlex — authoritative German text and multilingual record

textAvailability.note: All 77 Articles and two Annexes are stored in aligned English, German, French and Italian. German, French and Italian are equally authoritative; the default English display is official but has no legal force.
